name: Push-ups
description: A foundational upper body exercise that builds chest, shoulder, and tricep strength using only your bodyweight.
instructions: Start in a high plank position with hands shoulder-width apart
Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels
Lower your chest to the floor by bending your elbows at 45 degrees
Push back up to the starting position, fully extending your arms
Keep your core engaged throughout the movement

name: Squats
description: The king of lower body exercises. Squats build quad, glute, and hamstring strength while improving functional mobility.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly turned out
Keep your chest up and core braced throughout
Push your hips back and bend your knees to lower down
Lower until your thighs are parallel to the floor or below
Drive through your heels to return to standing

name: Deadlifts
description: A compound movement that trains the entire posterior chain — from your upper back down to your hamstrings and glutes.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart, weight over mid-foot
Hinge at the hips, keeping your back flat and chest up
Grip the bar just outside your legs with arms vertical
Take a deep breath and brace your core before lifting
Drive through your heels, extending hips and knees simultaneously
Lock out at the top with hips fully extended, then lower with control

name: Bicep Curls
description: An isolation exercise targeting the biceps brachii. Essential for building arm strength and definition.
instructions: Stand tall with feet shoulder-width apart, dumbbells at your sides
Keep your upper arms stationary against your torso
Curl the weights up by contracting your biceps
Raise until dumbbells are at shoulder level
Slowly lower back to the starting position with control
common_mistakes: Using momentum to swing the weight
